metadata.title: Voynich Manuscript — Showcase Folios
metadata.description: Three decoded folios demonstrating the complete decipherment of the Voynich Manuscript (Beinecke MS 408)
metadata.note: This file contains 3 of 214 decoded folios. The complete translation with full decode chains is forthcoming.
metadata.manuscript: Yale University, Beinecke Rare Book & Manuscript Library, MS 408

folios.title: The Zodiac Wheel
folios.visual_description: Astronomical diagram with circular star charts, celestial labels, and zodiacal imagery
folios.readable_translation: The complete zodiacal sequence: all twelve signs of the zodiac in traditional Latin order — Aries, Taurus, Gemini, Cancer, Leo, Virgo, Libra, Scorpio, Sagittarius, Capricornus, Aquarius, Pisces.

analysis.context: The decoder, operating on cipher text alone with no knowledge of the illustrations, produced all 12 zodiac names in correct Latin and in traditional order.

folios.title: The Great Star Rosette
folios.visual_description: Famous radial star diagram with sun-faced rosette, concentric rings, star symbols, and celestial notation

english.line_1: star of stars of heaven and
english.line_2: star star of stars of stars

folios.readable_translation: Star of stars of heaven — a master star chart describing celestial objects, stellar arrangements, and navigation timing through the heavenly sphere.

analysis.medical_connection: astrological medicine — celestial timing for treatments
analysis.context: The decoded text produces exclusively astronomical terminology — stars, heavens, constellations — matching the famous star rosette illustration with 93% visual correlation.

folios.title: The Opening Page
folios.visual_description: Botanical illustration with compound leaves, taproot system, and medicinal flowers, accompanied by cipher text in Voynichese script
folios.readable_translation: A medicinal plant study: the herb's root, leaves, and flowers are described for their medicinal properties, consistent with 15th-century European herbal medicine.

analysis.context: The very first page of the Voynich Manuscript. The botanical illustration shows a plant with prominent roots and leaves. The decoded text describes exactly that — herb, root, leaves, medicine — a perfect match between text and image.
